title: eriogonum leucophyllum subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum leucophyllum subsp. typicum, commonly known as Texas Sage, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Texas Sage include reducing stress and anxiety, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting respiratory health, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Texas Sage can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
